title: Tennessee vs Virginia March Madness Game Time, TV Details Announced for NCAA Tournament Clash
body:
The stage is set for an electrifying showdown in the NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament, as the Tennessee Volunteers are set to face off against the Virginia Cavaliers in the second round of March Madness. The highly anticipated matchup will take place on Sunday in Philadelphia, with the Vols seeking to upset the heavily favored Cavaliers and secure a spot in the Sweet 16.

The Volunteers, seeded sixth in the tournament with a record of 23-11, will be looking to capitalize on their momentum from a thrilling win in the first round. Meanwhile, the Cavaliers, ranked third in the tournament with a remarkable 30-5 record, will be seeking to continue their dominant form and move one step closer to a deep run in the championship. The two teams will face off on Sunday evening at 6:10 p.m. ET, with the game set to be broadcast live on TNT.

As the two teams prepare to clash on the court, fans are eagerly anticipating a contest that promises to be a true test of strength and skill. The Cavaliers' impressive regular season record has been built on a foundation of defensive prowess, with the team boasting one of the stingiest units in the nation. Conversely, the Volunteers have been known for their high-powered offense, which has consistently provided a spark for their team.

In this pivotal matchup, Tennessee head coach Rick Barnes will be looking to exploit any weaknesses in the Cavaliers' defense, while also attempting to mitigate the impact of Virginia's stifling unit on his own team's offense. Meanwhile, Cavaliers coach Tony Bennett will be seeking to utilize his team's exceptional defensive skills to limit the Vols' scoring opportunities and dictate the pace of the game.

This highly anticipated matchup also has significant implications for both teams' long-term prospects. For the Volunteers, a win would not only secure a spot in the Sweet 16 but also potentially propel them into the national conversation as a legitimate contender in the tournament. Conversely, a loss would put a damper on their momentum and raise questions about their ability to compete with the best teams in the nation.

In the end, Sunday's matchup will be a true test of wills and determination for both teams. As the Volunteers and Cavaliers take to the court in Philadelphia, fans can expect a thrilling contest that will be decided by the smallest of margins. With the stakes high and the competition fierce, only time will tell which team will emerge victorious and take the next step in their pursuit of the championship.
